text: Working with APIs allows applications to communicate and share data. When making an API call, you send an HTTP request, such as GET to retrieve information or POST to create new data. The response often comes in JSON format, which is easy for both humans and machines to read. Most APIs require authentication, usually through an API key, to ensure only authorised users can access the service. It's also important to follow rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a given time. Finally, always include error handling in your code to manage problems like network issues or invalid responses gracefully.
